ogTitle: Quinns Rocks vs Rangeway: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Quinns Rocks and Rangeway.
heading: Quinns Rocks vs Rangeway — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Quinns Rocks, Western Australia and Rangeway, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rangeway has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Quinns Rocks (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Quinns Rocks or Rangeway safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Quinns Rocks has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Rangeway scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Quinns Rocks scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Rangeway scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Quinns Rocks or Rangeway?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rangeway has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
